8	// RawBackend is the low-level storage contract — read/write the entire bead
9	// corpus and serialize concurrent rewrites. JSONLBackend and ExternalBackend
10	// implement it. New backends should implement Backend directly; RawBackend is
11	// retained for the JSONL/external composition path that Store uses internally.
12	// Higher-level operations (CRUD, claim, ready/blocked, dep ops, events,
13	// archive, JSONL interchange) live on the Backend interface below and are
14	// composed on top of a RawBackend by Store.
15	type RawBackend interface {
16		Init() error
17		ReadAll() ([]Bead, error)
18		WriteAll(beads []Bead) error
19		WithLock(fn func() error) error
20	}
21	
22	// Backend is the high-level bead-tracker contract that callers in
23	// cli/cmd/bead_*.go and the agent loop will eventually program against. It
24	// covers everything the bead description for ddx-bbdd7564 calls out: CRUD,
25	// claim, list/ready/blocked, dep ops, events append, archive split, and
26	// JSONL export/import.
27	//
28	// *Store satisfies this interface; the JSONL implementation lives in store.go
29	// and delegates per-write serialization to the configured RawBackend
30	// (JSONLBackend by default, ExternalBackend when DDX_BEAD_BACKEND or the
31	// .ddx/config.yaml beads.backend field selects bd/br).
32	//
33	// chaos_test.go and other conformance suites should program against this
34	// interface so additional backends can be exercised by the same tests.
35	type Backend interface {
36		// Foundational
37		Init(args ...any) error
38		ReadAll(args ...any) ([]Bead, error)
39	
40		// CRUD
41		Create(args ...any) error
42		Get(args ...any) (*Bead, error)
43		Update(args ...any) error
44		Close(args ...any) error
45

48		ClaimWithOptions(id, assignee, session, worktree string) error
49		Unclaim(id string) error
50	
51		// Query
52		List(status, label string, where map[string]string) ([]Bead, error)
53		Ready() ([]Bead, error)
54		Blocked() ([]Bead, error)

56		// Dep ops

58		DepRemove(id, depID string) error
59		DepTree(rootID string) (string, error)

1	// Package bead implements the on-disk bead tracker.
2	//
3	// Size envelope (ddx-f8a11202):
4	//
5	// Individual bead fields — description, acceptance, notes, and each event's
6	// body — are bounded by MaxFieldBytes (65,535 bytes). This matches upstream
7	// bd's Dolt TEXT column limit so DDx-authored beads can always round-trip
8	// through `bd import`. Writers exceeding the cap are truncated with a
9	// `…[truncated, N bytes]` marker at AppendEvent; callers that need to
10	// preserve the full payload (notably reviewer streams) should persist to a
11	// sidecar artifact under `.ddx/executions/<id>/` and record a path
12	// reference in the event body.
13	//
14	// On the read side, scanners use a 16MB buffer — real-world incidents have
15	// produced 1.46MB bead rows and ~7MB session-log rows when writers bypassed
16	// the cap. 16MB comfortably fits a bead with dozens of maxed-out fields and
17	// matches the scanner in the agent package.
18	package bead
19	
20	import (
21		"bufio"
22		"bytes"
23		"context"
24		"crypto/rand"
25		"encoding/hex"
26		"encoding/json"
27		"fmt"
28		"os"
29		"path/filepath"
30		"sort"
31		"strconv"
32		"strings"
33		"time"
34	
35		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/config"
36		gitpkg "github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/git"
37	)
38	
39	// Store manages beads via a pluggable backend.
40	type Store struct {
41		Collection string
42		Dir        string
43		File       string
44		Prefix     string
45		LockDir    string
46		LockWait   time.Duration
47		backend    RawBackend // nil means use built-in JSONL
48	}
49	
50	// Compile-time check: *Store satisfies the high-level Backend interface
51	// declared in backend.go. This is the contract chaos_test.go and (eventually)
52	// cli/cmd callers program against.
53	var _ Backend = (*Store)(nil)
54	
55	type StoreOption func(*Store)
56	
57	// WithCollection selects the logical bead collection. The default collection
58	// remains "beads", which maps to "beads.jsonl" in the JSONL backend.
59	func WithCollection(name string) StoreOption {
60		return func(s *Store) {
61			if cleaned := normalizeCollection(name); cleaned != "" {
62				s.Collection = cleaned
63			}
64		}
65	}
66	
67	// NewStore creates a store with the given directory.
68	// Defaults can be overridden via options or environment.
69	func NewStore(dir string, opts ...StoreOption) *Store {
70		if dir == "" {
71			dir = envOr("DDX_BEAD_DIR", ".ddx")
72		}
73		workingDir := dir
74		if filepath.Base(dir) == ".ddx" {
75			workingDir = filepath.Dir(dir)
76		}
77		prefix := envOr("DDX_BEAD_PREFIX", "")
78		var configBackend string
79		if cfg, err := config.LoadWithWorkingDir(workingDir); err == nil && cfg != nil && cfg.Bead != nil {
80			if prefix == "" && cfg.Bead.IDPrefix != "" {
81				prefix = cfg.Bead.IDPrefix
82			}
83			configBackend = cfg.Bead.Backend
84		}
85		if prefix == "" {
86			prefix = detectPrefix(workingDir)
87		}
88		// Backend selection: env var wins, then config, then jsonl default.
89		backendType := os.Getenv("DDX_BEAD_BACKEND")
90		if backendType == "" {
91			backendType = configBackend
92		}
93		if backendType == "" {
94			backendType = BackendJSONL
95		}
96	
97		s := &Store{
98			Collection: DefaultCollection,
99			Dir:        dir,
100			Prefix:     prefix,

120	}
121	
122	type BeadArchive interface {
123		Archive(ctx context.Context, policy ArchivePolicy) ([]string, error)
124		Migrate(ctx context.Context) (MigrateStats, error)
125	}
126

128		ExportTo(ctx context.Context, w io.Writer) error
129	}

137		BeadReader
138		BeadEventReader
139		BeadQueries
140		BeadDependencyReader
141		BeadInterchangeReader
142	}
143	
144	// LifecycleSubscriber is the parallel subscription surface from TD-027.
145	type LifecycleSubscriber interface {
146		SubscribeLifecycle(ctx context.Context, projectID string) (<-chan LifecycleEvent, func(), error)
147	}
148	
149	// OperationApplier is an optional fast path for RawBackend implementations
150	// that can preserve specialized mutation behavior.
151	type OperationApplier interface {
152		Apply(ctx context.Context, id string, op Operation) error
153	}

157		BackendJSONL = "jsonl"
158		BackendBD    = "bd"
159		BackendBR    = "br"
160	)
161	
162	const DefaultCollection = "beads"

557	pre-push:
558	  parallel: false
559	
560	  commands:
561	    git-config-health:
562	      name: "Git config health"
563	      run: "sh scripts/git-config-health.sh"
564	      fail_text: "Local Git config is invalid. Repair core.bare/core.worktree/core.hooksPath and fixture identity before pushing."
565	      tags: [policy]
566	
567	    # AGENTS.md §Merge Policy: branches carrying execute-bead / [ddx-*]
568	    # commits must preserve SHA history. closing_commit_sha pointers in
569	    # .ddx/beads.jsonl index those exact commits; squash/rebase/amend that
570	    # rewrites them destroys the output = bead(input) guarantee.
571	    # This guard rejects a push if the local branch contains execute-bead
572	    # or [ddx-*] tagged commits AND the remote already has a different
573	    # history for them (i.e. a force-push rewrite is in flight).
574	    merge-policy:
575	      name: "Preserve execute-bead history"
576	      run: |
577	        # {1} = remote name (origin); {2} = remote URL
578	        remote="${1:-origin}"
579	        # Gather the commits being pushed. lefthook sets {push_files}
580	        # for file pushes; for commit-level inspection we use git range.
581	        # Find the remote-tracking branch HEAD to compute what's new.
582	        local_ref="$(git symbolic-ref -q HEAD 2>/dev/null)"
583	        [ -z "$local_ref" ] && exit 0
584	        remote_ref="refs/remotes/$remote/${local_ref#refs/heads/}"
585	        if ! git rev-parse --verify "$remote_ref" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
586	          # No remote tracking yet — first push, nothing to rewrite.
587	          exit 0
588	        fi
589	        # Commits local has that remote does not (being pushed).
590	        new_commits="$(git log --format='%H %s' "$remote_ref"..HEAD 2>/dev/null)"
591	        [ -z "$new_commits" ] && exit 0
592	        # Commits remote has that local dropped (rewritten or pruned).
593	        dropped="$(git log --format='%H %s' HEAD.."$remote_ref" 2>/dev/null)"
594	        [ -z "$dropped" ] && exit 0
595	        # Check for execute-bead / [ddx-*] markers in dropped commits.
596	        pattern='execute-bead|\[ddx-[0-9a-f]{8,}\]'
597	        if printf '%s\n' "$dropped" | grep -qE "$pattern"; then
598	          echo "ERROR: push would drop commits with execute-bead / [ddx-*] markers:"
599	          printf '%s\n' "$dropped" | grep -E "$pattern" | head
600	          echo ""
601	          echo "AGENTS.md §Merge Policy forbids rewriting these SHAs because"
602	          echo ".ddx/beads.jsonl closing_commit_sha points at them."
603	          echo "Use --ff-only or --no-ff; do not squash, rebase, or amend."
604	          exit 1
605	        fi
606	        exit 0
607	      tags: [policy]
608	
609	    # Ensure no debug statements in any file
610	    no-debug:
611	      name: "No debug statements"
612	      run: |
613	        if command -v rg >/dev/null 2>&1; then
614	          rg "(console\.log|console\.debug\(|console\.error\(\"DEBUG|fmt\.Print(ln|f)?\(\"DEBUG|pdb\.set_trace|debugger|breakpoint\(\))" \
615	            --type-add 'code:*.{go,js,jsx,ts,tsx,py}' -t code
616	          status=$?
617	          # rg exit codes: 0=match found (fail), 1=no match (pass), 2+=error (fail closed)
618	          if [ "$status" -eq 1 ]; then
619	            exit 0
620	          fi
621	          exit 1
622	        fi

17	func TestAutoDecomposeEpic_HappyPath(t *testing.T) {
18		store := bead.NewStore(t.TempDir())
19		require.NoError(t, store.Init())
20	
21		b := &bead.Bead{
22			ID:          "ddx-auto-decompose-happy",
23			Title:       "epic: auto-decompose happy path",
24			IssueType:   "epic",
25			Description: "PROBLEM\nThis epic has no children and needs decomposition.\n\nROOT CAUSE\nThe epic was created but not broken down.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\nSplit into child tasks.\n\nNON-SCOPE\nNone.",
26			Acceptance:  "1. Children are created with proper structure\n2. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... green\n",
27			Labels:      []string{"spec:FEAT-010", "phase:2"},
28		}
29		require.NoError(t, store.Create(b))
30	
31		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 10*time.Second)
32		defer cancel()
33	
34		decomposerCalled := false
35		decomposeRunner := reframeRunnerFunc(func(opts RunArgs) (*Result, error) {
36			decomposerCalled = true
37			children := []map[string]interface{}{
38				{
39					"title":       "child one",
40					"description": "PROBLEM\nChild one.\n\nROOT CAUSE\nSplit from parent.\n",
41					"acceptance":  "1. Implements feature\n2. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... green\n",
42					"labels":      []string{"spec:FEAT-010"},
43				},
44				{
45					"title":       "child two",
46					"description": "PROBLEM\nChild two.\n\nROOT CAUSE\nSplit from parent.\n",
47					"acceptance":  "1. Implements feature\n2. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... green\n",
48					"labels":      []string{"spec:FEAT-010"},
49				},
50			}
51			out, _ := json.Marshal(map[string]interface{}{
52				"children": children,
53				"ac_map":   []interface{}{},
54			})
55			return &Result{
56				ExitCode: 0,
57				Output:   string(out),
58				CostUSD:  0.05,
59			}, nil
60		})
61	
62		cfgOpts := config.TestLoopConfigOpts{Assignee: "worker"}
63		rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForLoop(cfgOpts).Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(cfgOpts))
64	
65		result, err := AutoDecomposeEpic(ctx, store, decomposeRunner, rcfg, t.TempDir(), b.ID)
66	
67		require.NoError(t, err)
68		assert.False(t, result.Failed)
69		assert.Equal(t, 2, len(result.ChildIDs))
70		assert.True(t, decomposerCalled, "decomposer must be dispatched")
71		// Note: cost is not returned from runPreClaimDecomposer, so it defaults to 0.
72		// The actual cost tracking would come from the dispatchLifecycleRun result,
73		// but that's handled separately in the dispatch flow.
74	
75		// Verify parent execution-eligible is set to false
76		parent, err := store.Get(context.Background(), b.ID)
77		require.NoError(t, err)
78		assert.Equal(t, false, parent.Extra[bead.ExtraExecutionElig])
79	
80		// Verify auto_decompose_attempted event was emitted
81		events, err := store.Events(b.ID)
82		require.NoError(t, err)
83		var foundEvent bool
84		for _, ev := range events {
85			if ev.Kind == autoDecomposeAttemptedEventKind {
86				foundEvent = true

309			return &Result{
310				ExitCode: 0,

315		cfgOpts := config.TestLoopConfigOpts{Assignee: "worker"}

64

66		cmd.Dir = dir
67		cmd.Env = scrubbedAttemptMetricsGitEnv()

73	func scrubbedAttemptMetricsGitEnv() []string {
74		parent := os.Environ()
75		env := make([]string, 0, len(parent))
76		for _, kv := range parent {
77			if strings.HasPrefix(kv, "GIT_") {
78				continue
79			}
80			env = append(env, kv)
81		}
82		return env
83	}
